duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Broken onlyOwner/onlyManager modifiers in Security.sol evaluate boolean expressions as no-op statements without require/revert, making all protected functions publicly callable and enabling complete protocol takeover
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same root defect: Security.sol modifiers are no-ops. TECH-H013/OPS-H004/OPS-H013 focus on fee routing consequences, TECH2-H003(2nd batch) on protocol takeover, but all share the identical code defect in Security.sol lines 8-15.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Fee manipulation to ≥100% via broken access control causes arithmetic underflow in sell calculations, permanently blocking all sell transactions and trapping user funds
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same compound exploit: broken AC modifiers allow anyone to set maxFeePercent high then set fees ≥100%, causing sellValue underflow in _transferFees. ECON-H004 has the most comprehensive impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Broken access control allows anyone to replace the ERC20 factory via setERC20Factory, enabling deployment of malicious ERC20 tokens that break the 1:1 backing invariant
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same exploit: broken onlyOwner allows replacing curvesERC20Factory with a malicious factory that deploys backdoored ERC20 tokens. Same code path (setERC20Factory → _deployERC20), same impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: FeeSplitter.setCurves() has no access control modifier, allowing anyone to point it to a malicious contract returning fabricated balances/supply, draining all ETH via claimFees
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the identical vulnerability: setCurves() is public with zero access control, enabling dependency hijack of FeeSplitter's balance/supply data source. TECH2-H004 (2nd batch) is the same as others. ECON-H002 has the best attack economics analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Internal token transfers (_transfer via transferCurvesToken/transferAllCurvesTokens) do not call feeRedistributor.onBalanceChange, leaving recipient's userFeeOffset at 0 and enabling retroactive claims of all historical fees
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same defect: _transfer updates curvesTokenBalance without calling feeRedistributor.onBalanceChange for sender or receiver. Recipient offset=0 allows claiming historical fees. TECH2-H003(1st) and TECH2-H008(2nd) are the same vulnerability. ECON-H003 best describes the insolvency cascade.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: FeeSplitter.onBalanceChange overwrites userFeeOffset without calling updateFeeCredit first, permanently destroying accumulated unclaimed fees — occurs passively on every buy/sell AND can be weaponized via broken onlyManager
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All share the same root code defect: onBalanceChange sets userFeeOffset=cumulativeFeePerToken without calling updateFeeCredit. Some focus on passive loss during normal buys (TECH-H005, ECON-H008), others on active exploitation via broken AC (TECH-H015, ECON-H005), and some on both (OPS-H005). TECH2-H004(1st) is passive, TECH2-H005(2nd) is both. TECH-H004 is CRITICAL and clearly describes the weaponized exploit.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Protocol fee and referral fee (when undefined) on sell transactions are deducted from seller payout but never forwarded to destinations, permanently locking ETH in Curves contract with no recovery mechanism
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same _transferFees sell path accounting bug: sellValue deducts protocolFee but never sends it to protocolFeeDestination; referralFee deducted but skipped when undefined. Some focus on protocol fee only (TECH-H006), some combine both (TECH-H017, OPS-H007). TECH2-H005(1st) is protocol-fee-only, TECH2-H007(2nd) is combined. OPS-H007 comprehensively describes both issues.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Referral fee on sell transactions is deducted from seller's payout but the .call is skipped when referralFeeDestination is address(0), permanently locking the ETH
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same specific bug: referral fee deducted from sellValue but (true, bytes('')) skips transfer when no referral defined. These focus exclusively on the referral fee aspect (vs DUP-007 which includes combined threats). TECH2-H006(1st batch) is the same issue.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: All circulating tokens wrapped to ERC20 via withdraw() causes FeeSplitter.totalSupply to return 0, making addFees revert with NoTokenHolders and halting all buy/sell operations
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same DoS mechanism: withdraw reduces FeeSplitter.totalSupply (which excludes Curves-held tokens), reaching 0 causes addFees revert, blocking _transferFees and thus all trading. TECH2-H006(2nd batch) is the same. TECH-H007 has the clearest technical analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Withdraw/deposit cycle exploits stale fee offsets — withdraw reduces internal balance without updating FeeSplitter, fees accumulate at higher per-token rate, deposit restores balance, user claims inflated fees retroactively
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same exploit: withdraw/deposit don't call onBalanceChange, so userFeeOffset stays stale while totalSupply changes, enabling retroactive fee claims for periods when user held 0 internal tokens. TECH2-H007(1st batch) is the same. TECH-H008 has the clearest step-by-step exploit description.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Excess ETH from buy overpayment (msg.value > price + totalFee) is permanently locked in Curves contract with no refund mechanism
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same defect: _buyCurvesToken checks msg.value >= price+totalFee but never refunds excess. Same code location, same impact (permanently locked ETH).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Any token holder can trigger ERC20 deployment via withdraw() bypassing onlyTokenSubject modifier on mint(), forcing deployment with default metadata and permanently locking the subject out of custom branding
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same bypass: withdraw() calls _deployERC20 directly without onlyTokenSubject, allowing any holder to force ERC20 deployment with default name/symbol. TECH2-H010 appears in both batches with same vulnerability. TECH-H011 is the most technically precise.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: getPrice arithmetic underflow when supply=0 and amount>1 due to (supply-1+amount) evaluating supply-1 first in Solidity 0.8.7 checked arithmetic, preventing multi-token initial purchase
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the identical arithmetic issue: getPrice sum2 calculation underflows when supply=0 and amount>1 because (0-1) is evaluated before +amount. TECH2-H009(1st batch) is the same. TECH-H019 has the clearest technical description.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: FeeSplitter.onBalanceChange pushes tokens to userTokens array without duplicate check on every call, causing unbounded array growth that DoS's getUserTokensAndClaimable and batchClaiming
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same defect: userTokens[account].push(token) in onBalanceChange has no duplicate check, growing unboundedly. TECH2-H008(1st batch) is the same. TECH-H012 best describes both the mechanism and DoS impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Buying 0 tokens is not prevented — getPrice returns 0 for amount=0, allowing zero-cost transactions that trigger state updates and array bloat in ownedCurvesTokenSubjects and FeeSplitter.userTokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing validation: no require(amount>0) in _buyCurvesToken allows zero-cost state manipulation. TECH2-H009(2nd batch) describes the same issue. TECH-H020 has the most comprehensive analysis.
